executive_summary.body: Project Meridian (PE buyout, Industrials/US). Deal-team recommendation: conditional-approve. Entry 8.0x EBITDA on EV 480.0; base case 2.4x / 21.0% IRR over 5 years. This is a draft for committee decision.

investment_thesis.body: Recurring aftermarket revenue is 55% of sales with high retention. Fragmented sector supports a buy-and-build add-on strategy. Quality-of-earnings confirmed normalized EBITDA of 60.0.

transaction_structure.body: EV 480.0 at 8.0x EBITDA (60.0). Net debt 288.0; equity check 192.0. Total debt 300.0 = 5.0x EBITDA.

valuation.body: EV/EBITDA vs. peers: entry 8.0x vs. peer range 7.5x-9.5x.

returns_analysis.body: Base case 2.4x MOIC / 21.0% IRR over 5 years.

scenario_analysis.body: Base MOIC 2.4x / IRR 21.0%; Upside MOIC 3.1x / IRR 28.0%; Downside MOIC 1.3x / IRR 6.0%

key_risks_and_mitigants.body: [medium] Customer concentration: top 3 customers are 34% of revenue -> Mitigant: Multi-year contracts; cross-sell into aftermarket. [medium] Cyclical end-market exposure could compress the exit multiple -> Mitigant: Downside case models 7.0x exit and still returns 1.3x. [low] Pending environmental permit at the Ohio facility -> Mitigant: Escrow and seller indemnity in the SPA.

position_sizing_and_portfolio_fit.body: Proposed commitment 60.0 on fund NAV 1200.0 = 5.0% (single-name limit 8.0%). Sector exposure post-deal 19.0% vs. limit 25.0%.

recommendation_and_decision_questions.body: Deal-team recommendation: conditional-approve (committee decision pending). Decision questions: (1) Do we approve a commitment of up to $60mm at 8.0x entry? (2) Is the proposed 5.0x leverage acceptable given the cyclical end-market? (3) Do we require the environmental escrow as a condition precedent? (4) Is the buy-and-build add-on pipeline sufficient to underwrite the upside case?

assertions.claim: Recurring aftermarket revenue is 55% of sales with high retention
Fragmented sector supports a buy-and-build add-on strategy
Customer concentration: top 3 customers are 34% of revenue
Cyclical end-market exposure could compress the exit multiple
Pending environmental permit at the Ohio facility

decision_questions: Do we approve a commitment of up to $60mm at 8.0x entry?
Is the proposed 5.0x leverage acceptable given the cyclical end-market?
Do we require the environmental escrow as a condition precedent?
Is the buy-and-build add-on pipeline sufficient to underwrite the upside case?
standing_note: Draft investment-committee memorandum for human review. It is not investment advice, records no committee decision, and has not been circulated, sent, or submitted.
